title: Bestselling Authors
description: You are given four tables: authors, books, stores, and sales records. Find names of authors whose books have sold more than 1000 copies in total across all stores, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
authors(id, name, genre)
books(id, title, author_id)
stores(id, name, city)
sales(id, book_id, store_id, quantity)
outputFormat: One author name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use three JOINs and GROUP BY. Use HAVING SUM(quantity) > 1000. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT a.name FROM authors a JOIN books b ON a.id = b.author_id JOIN sales s ON b.id = s.book_id GROUP BY a.id, a.name HAVING SUM(s.quantity) > 1000 ORDER BY a.name;
hint: Join authors → books → sales (skip stores unless needed), GROUP BY author, HAVING SUM(quantity) > 1000.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE authors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, author_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE stores (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, store_id INTEGER, quantity INTEGER); INSERT INTO authors VALUES (1,'Alice','Sci-Fi'),(2,'Bob','Mystery'),(3,'Carol','Fantasy'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'Book One',1),(2,'Book Two',1),(3,'Book Three',2),(4,'Book Four',3); INSERT INTO stores VALUES (1,'StoreA','NY'),(2,'StoreB','LA'); INSERT INTO sales VALUES (1,1,1,600),(2,1,2,500),(3,2,1,200),(4,3,1,300),(5,4,1,400),(6,4,2,700);

CREATE TABLE authors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, author_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE stores (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, store_id INTEGER, quantity INTEGER); INSERT INTO authors VALUES (1,'Dan','Romance'),(2,'Eve','Thriller'),(3,'Frank','Non-Fiction'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'Dan A',1),(2,'Dan B',1),(3,'Eve A',2),(4,'Frank A',3); INSERT INTO stores VALUES (1,'ShopX','Boston'),(2,'ShopY','Chicago'); INSERT INTO sales VALUES (1,1,1,400),(2,1,2,400),(3,2,1,300),(4,3,1,500),(5,3,2,600),(6,4,1,100);

CREATE TABLE authors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, author_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE stores (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, store_id INTEGER, quantity INTEGER); INSERT INTO authors VALUES (1,'Grace','Horror'),(2,'Hank','Comedy'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'Scary Tales',1),(2,'Laughs',2); INSERT INTO stores VALUES (1,'BookShop','Denver'); INSERT INTO sales VALUES (1,1,1,500),(2,2,1,400);

CREATE TABLE authors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, author_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE stores (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, store_id INTEGER, quantity INTEGER); INSERT INTO authors VALUES (1,'Iris','Drama'),(2,'Jack','Poetry'),(3,'Kate','SciFi'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'Iris Book',1),(2,'Jack Book',2),(3,'Kate Book',3); INSERT INTO stores VALUES (1,'Store1','NYC'),(2,'Store2','LA'); INSERT INTO sales VALUES (1,1,1,800),(2,1,2,500),(3,2,1,600),(4,2,2,600),(5,3,1,700),(6,3,2,400);

CREATE TABLE authors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, author_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE stores (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, store_id INTEGER, quantity INTEGER); INSERT INTO authors VALUES (1,'Leo','Adventure'),(2,'Mia','Romance'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'Leo Vol 1',1),(2,'Mia Vol 1',2); INSERT INTO stores VALUES (1,'MainStore','Houston'); INSERT INTO sales VALUES (1,1,1,1000),(2,2,1,1001);
